name: Carta Di Musica
recipeIngredient: 1 Ounce fresh yeast or 2 packets dry yeast
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, stir together 1 cup flour, 1/2 cup semolina, and salt.
2. In a measuring cup, mix yeast in 3/4 cup water and stir to dissolve.
3. Mix the liquid with the flour mixture to form doughy batter. Let sit covered overnight in the warmest place in the kitchen.
4. Preheat oven to 450 degrees F.
5. Make a well on a work surface using 1 cup flour and 1/2 cup semolina. Place the doughy batter in the center and stir in the remaining warm water. Mix well and incorporate flour from the edges of the well until a dough is formed. Bring together and knead for 10 minutes, until firm and elastic. Place in a bowl, cover with a towel, and let rest for 1 hour. The dough should double in size.
6. Cut the dough into tennis ball-sized pieces. Using a rolling pin, roll out to 12 to 14-inch circles, about 1/8th to 1/4 inch thin. Place on a peel and slide onto oven tiles.
7. Bake for 2 minutes. Open the oven door and turn over each piece. Cook for an additional 30 seconds and remove to a board. The bread should be puffed up like a balloon.
8. Immediately cut around the edges with a knife to separate the top and bottom layers.
9. Stack the two pieces on a kitchen towel and put a box of cereal on top to weigh them down. Repeat this process until all the dough is cut, baked, separated, and stacked.
10. Place each half back into the oven for 15 to 20 seconds at a time, until very crisp. Store wrapped in a brown paper bag. They will last three to four weeks in a dry place.
